_comment: Cross-language STATELESS per-recipe validation contract (ADR-024 §S5). The Python compiler (shape.py recipe_id + parse_threshold) and the Go exporter/preflight (resolveOneCustomAlert) MUST AGREE on accept/reject for each spec — this closes the validation-decision drift that the slug golden vectors (recipe_id_vectors.json) did NOT cover. Scope = validations BOTH perform: recipe/metric/op/horizon/selector-reserved/severity/for enums + adversarial selector-VALUE acceptance (key-valid value accepted both sides; Python _escape_value is the injection boundary at emit, not the validator). NOT here: Go-stricter preflight extras (NaN/Inf threshold, mode enum, ratio-floor) — tested per-language; and STATEFUL checks (name/severity uniqueness, cap, cross-tree) — also per-language mock-tree tests.

cases._note: valid threshold
valid forecast (ratio mode, in-range floor)
adversarial selector VALUE accepted (key valid; value escaped at emit by Python _escape_value, never breaks the quoted literal)
bad metric (colon → recording-rule reference)
unknown recipe
bad op
op == on threshold accepted (#810: status/error-code-as-value match on a raw gauge)
op == rejected on a computed-value recipe (float-fragile; threshold-recipe-only gate)
op == rejected on ratio (otherwise-valid spec; the gate is the sole rejection reason)
op == rejected on p99_latency (otherwise-valid spec)
op == rejected on forecast raw mode (otherwise-valid spec; gate fires before horizon shaping)

op == rejected on absence too (#819 adversarial: op is meaningless for a presence check; the gate now rejects it in lockstep with the schema if/then editor-guard, so no front/back brain-split on API/GitOps-submitted specs)
forecast bad horizon (not enum)
reserved selector label
bad severity
bad for (not enum)

F2: selector VALUE carrying a Go-template action rejected — it reaches the alert-annotation template context (recipes.py _alert_rule) where Prometheus evaluates {{ query ... }} across the whole TSDB; _escape_value defends only the PromQL string-literal, not the template. PortSwigger logic-less principle: untrusted data must never become template code.
F2: selector VALUE with a backtick (Go raw-string delimiter; survives _escape_value which only escapes \ " \n) rejected

F1: window with a PromQL injection rejected — window is raw-interpolated into rate(m[<window>]) (recipes.py); must match the Go-duration charset ^([0-9]+(ns|us|µs|ms|s|m|h))+$ (the schema pattern, now enforced imperatively both sides)
F1: quantile with a PromQL injection rejected — quantile is raw-interpolated into histogram_quantile(<quantile>, ...) (recipes.py); must be a number in (0,1)

F1-lockstep (Phase C hunter): quantile as a Go hex-float literal. Go strconv.ParseFloat accepts "0x1p-1"=0.5 but CPython float() rejects it → without a shared decimal-only regex the Go preflight goes GREEN while the Python CI compiler goes RED, landing a poison commit that wedges the platform-wide #741 S3b drift gate. Must be REJECTED by BOTH (decimal-float charset).
